title: ageratina tovarae
intro: Ageratina tovarae, also known as Tovar's woodbetony, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family that is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ageratina tovarae include its ability to alleviate headaches, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties.
